assumptions: The declared intragroup dividend has created a recorded intragroup receivable/payable that is a monetary item.
The question concerns ordinary consolidated financial statements unless the investment-entity exception is specifically relevant.

approaches.label: Hedge of foreign currency risk on an intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale: IFRS 9 permits the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item to be a hedged item in consolidated financial statements if the related FX gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
conditions: The dividend receivable/payable is a monetary item between group entities.
The entities have different functional currencies.
The FX gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
The hedge relationship satisfies IFRS 9 designation, documentation and effectiveness requirements.

approaches.label: General exclusion of intragroup items in consolidation
approaches.rationale: As a general rule, only items with a party external to the reporting entity can be designated as hedged items in consolidated financial statements.
conditions: This general prohibition is overridden only by specific IFRS 9 exceptions.
A separate exception exists for investment entities for transactions with subsidiaries measured at fair value through profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of a forecast intragroup transaction
approaches.rationale: The forecast intragroup transaction exception applies only while the transaction is highly probable and affects consolidated profit or loss, not once the receivable has already been recorded.
conditions: It is denominated in a currency other than the functional currency of the entity entering into it.
The foreign currency risk affects consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale: IFRIC 16 addresses hedges of foreign currency risk on net assets of a foreign operation, which is a different hedge accounting model from hedging a recorded intragroup dividend receivable.
conditions: The hedged item is an amount of net assets of a foreign operation.
The hedged risk is between the functional currency of the foreign operation and that of a parent entity.
The same net investment exposure can qualify for hedge accounting only once in the ultimate parent's consolidated financial statements.
